findings.detail: Flowchart LR with main cycle Q->T->A->O, loop-back edge O->T labeled 'need more info', and terminal edge T->FA labeled 'task complete'. The cycle structure matches the ReAct pattern intent exactly: Thought is the decision node, Action triggers tool call, Observation feeds back. The dotted T -.- C1T anchor positions the TRACE subgraph below T without visual clutter.

All 8 node labels (Question, Thought x2, Action x2, Observation x2, Final Answer) map 1:1 to corpus §5.2 four-stage cycle and §5.5 Eiffel Tower trace. No bare acronyms — 'ReAct' appears only in the title: field where the corpus defines it. Edge labels 'need more info' and 'task complete' paraphrase corpus §5.2 ('loops back' and 'has enough information'). All bold labels ≤ 2 words.
Governing concept 'ReAct — Reasoning + Acting interleaved' lives in the config title: field. No ghost node is used for the central organizing idea.
No LangChain, no decision matrix, no agent safety nodes. All nodes trace to topics 14.1–14.6 vocabulary.

Question uses start (blue), Thought nodes use auto (purple) correctly identifying the LLM-generated reasoning step, Action and Observation use act (blue) for tool-related steps, Final Answer uses done (green). No HITL nodes are present. Color tiers are correctly applied.

preflight.title_discipline: pass (governing concept in title: field, no ghost node for central idea)
